title: Capstone: Ship a Production RAG System
concept: A final worked capstone combining every phase pillar into one deployable system. 'Phase G: LLM Application Engineering is complete. You have mastered the entire LLM Application Engineering curriculum!'.

exercises.prompt: Why do system architecture skills (like database indexing and retrieval) compound in value over time?

options.text: They quickly become obsolete whenever a new LLM version is released
They focus on data and context quality, which benefits any model you plug in
They rely entirely on memorizing specific model prompt biases

exercises.explain: Unlike brittle prompt hacks tailored to specific model quirks, architectural pillars like clean data chunking and smart retrieval remain valuable even when you upgrade the underlying LLM.

cards.title: The Ultimate Integration
cards.body: You have learned how to chunk data, retrieve context, evaluate answers, and write robust prompts. Now, it's time to connect all these pillars into a single deployable application ready for real users.

cards.title: Production RAG Systems
cards.body: A production RAG system integrates data ingestion, vector indexing, semantic retrieval, LLM prompting, and continuous evaluation into one reliable pipeline. It moves your system from a local prototype to a live, user-facing application.

cards.title: The Restaurant Launch
cards.body: Think of shipping a RAG system like opening a restaurant. Your vector database is the pantry, the LLM is the chef, evaluation is the food critic, and deployment is the grand opening. You must coordinate them all for a successful launch.

cards.title: Production in Action
cards.body: A company deploys a customer support bot by syncing their help desk articles to a vector database daily, retrieving top articles for user queries, routing them to an LLM, and monitoring response safety.

cards.title: The One-and-Done Myth
cards.body: Many believe that shipping RAG is a one-time setup. In reality, production systems require continuous monitoring, data updates, and drift evaluation to remain accurate over time.

cards.title: Prototype vs. Production
cards.body: Compare a fragile demo prototype with a robust, production-grade RAG architecture.

left.text: Hardcoded local files, single-turn prompts, no evaluation, manual runs.

right.text: Automated data pipelines, vector databases, real-time evaluation, and system monitoring.

exercises.prompt: In a production RAG architecture, what is the primary role of the Ingestion Pipeline?

options.text: It generates the final customer-facing conversational response
It chunks, embeds, and updates the knowledge source in the vector database
It acts as a firewall to block all user queries from reaching the LLM
It replaces the LLM entirely with static template files

exercises.explain: Ingestion acts as the pipeline that prepares and updates the source documents, ensuring the retrieval system always has access to the latest data.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Automated evaluation should run continuously in production, not just during development.
exercises.explain: Evaluation is critical at every stage of the lifecycle to measure how changes in chunking, prompts, or models affect accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the production best practice:
exercises.sentence: To keep your production RAG system running smoothly, you must set up continuous monitoring for user queries.
exercises.explain: Deploying a RAG system to production is just the start; you must set up continuous monitoring to track real-world errors and performance.

exercises.prompt: Identify the code snippet that represents an automated evaluation component of a production RAG system:

options.text: print('System is running fine on port 8080!')
def eval_rag(query, context, answer): return judge_llm.score(answer, context)
while True: user_input = input('Ask me anything: ')

exercises.explain: Option B mimics a real production evaluation script that compares retrieved contexts with generated answers to calculate faithfulness. Options A and C are generic templates.

exercises.prompt: To successfully ship and maintain a production RAG system, which architecture represents the complete end-to-end engineering lifecycle?

options.text: Connecting ingestion, vector retrieval, LLM generation, evaluation, and user feedback monitoring into a continuous loop
Relying on a single giant prompt that handles all steps without any retrieval database
Deploying a model once and never updating the database or checking user feedback
Building only the ingestion pipeline and leaving the LLM generation to the user's browser

exercises.explain: Shipping a production RAG system requires a continuous loop of ingesting clean data, serving it via fast retrieval, generating safe answers, and evaluating performance based on real user feedback. Phase G: LLM Application Engineering is complete. You have mastered the entire LLM Application Engineering curriculum!
